summary: First end-to-end test run of Sistem Penapisan CV (Semantic CV Screening). Backend: 21/21 pytest tests PASSED including auth, RBAC, dashboard, JD extraction (Emergent LLM), CV upload + background candidate processing, screening detail, decision update, AI provider listing+test, and user CRUD. Frontend: Login (with demo quick-fill), Dashboard, Jobs list, Job creation (via prior backend), Job detail with extracted must-have/nice-to-have criteria, candidate ranking, Admin Provider page with working Test Koneksi button, Admin Users page, and RBAC redirect from /admin to /dashboard for HR — all verified working. Bahasa Indonesia UI confirmed throughout.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking a candidate row in the 'Ranking Kandidat' table does NOT navigate to /screenings/{screening_id}. There is no <a href="/screenings/..."> link or row click handler — the row is plain. The ScreeningDetail page (with radar chart, dimension scores, rationale, strengths/gaps, and decision buttons) is implemented and routed at /screenings/:id, but users have no way to reach it from the UI. The 'Shortlist'/'Pending' text in the Keputusan column is just a badge, not a button.

issues: Clean, consistent design; sidebar nav + content layout works well at 1920px. No contrast or spacing issues observed in the flows tested.

action_items: JobDetail.jsx: wrap the candidate ranking <tr> in a Link to `/screenings/${row.id}` (only when row.id is present, i.e. screening completed). Without this, the entire ScreeningDetail experience (radar chart, rationale, decision buttons) is unreachable through normal UI navigation.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: create_job is documented as triggering 'background' extraction but actually runs extract_jd_criteria synchronously in the request handler (5-15s LLM call). Works but blocks the HTTP request; consider FastAPI BackgroundTasks for non-blocking UX, and have the frontend poll job.extraction_status. Not a bug — matches current frontend expectation.
server.py L223: `if not text or len(text.strip()) < 20` — minimum 20 chars is arbitrary; consider relaxing or making configurable.
auth.py L11: JWT_SECRET falls back to 'fallback-secret' if env var missing — should fail-fast in production rather than silently use a weak default.
server.py: ai_provider api_key masking uses len(d['api_key']) > 4 check but emergent default has empty api_key — already handled correctly.
models.py: JobPostingCreate is defined but unused — create_job endpoint uses Form() fields instead. Remove unused model or document.
server.py L289: list_jobs runs a separate count_documents per job in a loop — O(N) DB calls; for large job counts consider aggregation pipeline.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (1 UX gap: candidate row → screening detail navigation missing)

seed_data_creation: Created via tests: 1 job 'TEST Payroll Specialist' with auto-extracted criteria (4 must-have, 2 nice-to-have), 1 candidate 'Budi Santoso' (score 97, shortlisted), 1 extra user 'TEST Updated' (test_user_*@example.com). Decision on Budi was set to 'shortlisted'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid. Only remaining issue is the missing link from JobDetail candidate row → /screenings/:id. After fix, retest by: 1) login as HR, 2) navigate to Jobs → click TEST Payroll Specialist, 3) click Budi Santoso's row, 4) verify ScreeningDetail page loads with radar chart (recharts SVG), rationale text in Bahasa Indonesia, strengths/gaps lists, and 3 decision buttons (Shortlist/Hold/Reject) that update via PATCH /api/screenings/:id/decision. The existing seed job/candidate can be reused.
rca of the issue: JobDetail.jsx renders candidate rows as <tr> with no <Link> wrapper or onClick → useNavigate(`/screenings/${row.id}`). Even though the screening row has an `id` field returned from GET /api/jobs/:id/candidates, the UI never uses it to enable drill-down. Fix: in the rows map, when row.id is non-null (screening completed), wrap row contents in <Link to={`/screenings/${row.id}`}> or add row onClick navigation. Pending candidates (row.id === null) should remain non-clickable.
